---
title: "Frameworks"
subtitle: "What each framework specifies, where it sits, and what its source license permits"
listing:
  contents: "*-*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, jurisdiction, framework-type, organizing-units, elements, license]
  field-display-names:
    title: "Framework"
    jurisdiction: "Jurisdiction"
    framework-type: "Type"
    organizing-units: "Units"
    elements: "Elements"
    license: "License"
  sort: "framework-type asc, jurisdiction asc, title asc"
  sort-ui: false
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 10
  table-striped: true
  table-hover: true
---

The framework corpus splits along two axes: workforce versus
pedagogy, and US/EU/global jurisdiction. The combinations are not
exhaustive (there is no global pedagogy framework, and the US is
overrepresented in both columns), but together the frameworks span
the practical comparison surface for most cybersecurity workforce
and education research questions.

::: {.column-margin}
Counts on this table use the with-examples figure. Strict counts
(numbered standards only) appear on each framework's page and on
the [density-spread query](../queries/density-spread.qmd).
:::
